channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Mali’s Defense Minister Killed in Attacks by Islamist Insurgents
item.description: Mali's Defense Minister Gen Sadio Camara was killed in attacks by Islamist insurgents amid escalating regional violence. The death of the minister, Gen. Sadio Camara, a central figure in the country’s military government, comes amid escalating violence in the region.

item.title: Putin pledges support for Iran in talks with FM Araghchi
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin expressed support for Iran during a meeting with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, who is visiting Russia amid stalled US talks. Putin hoped for a swift return to peace.

item.title: Israel’s deadly attacks amid ceasefire breaches
item.description: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 14 people and displaced more as the ceasefire faltered. The attacks occurred amid ongoing breaches of the ceasefire. Israeli airstrikes hit southern Lebanon as ceasefire falters, killing at least 14 and forcing new displacement.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran Offers To Open Strait Of Hormuz, Gives US Conditions
item.description: Iran's minister arrived in Russia as ceasefire talks between the US, Israel, and Iran collapsed. Iran blamed the US for the collapse of the talks. US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran Blames US For Collapse Of Talks As Minister Arrives In Russia NDTV.

item.title: Iran’s FM Araghchi calls his Islamabad visit ‘successful'’ after arriving in Russia
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi visited Islamabad and described the trip as successful. He then traveled to Saint Petersburg for talks with Russian President Vladimir Putin. Meeting with Vladimir Putin will be a good opportunity to discuss war developments, says Iran FM Araghchi; Iran FM arrives in Saint Petersburg for Putin talks, says Iranian state media.

item.title: How does targeting water supply during war worsen the scarcity crisis?
item.description: Water infrastructure is being targeted in wars, worsening scarcity crises. We explore why water infrastructure is increasingly being targeted in the midst of war and conflict. How does targeting water supply during war worsen the scarcity crisis?

item.title: ICDS Hosts 2-day European Think-tank Network on China Meeting
item.description: The International Centre for Defence and Security hosted a two-day European Think-tank Network on China meeting. The event brought together experts to discuss China-related issues. ICDS Hosts 2-day European Think-tank Network on China Meeting International Centre for Defence and Security.

item.title: Hormuz Traffic Remains Dry Amid Chokehold, 7 Ships Cross Strait In 24 Hours
item.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remained low with seven ships crossing in 24 hours. The US Central Command reported redirecting 37 vessels since imposing a blockade on Iran on April 13.

item.title: Germany’s Merz says Iran ‘humiliated’ US in its war
item.description: German Chancellor Olaf Scholz's ally, Merz, said Iran humiliated the US in its war, warning the US risks becoming bogged down in another quagmire like Iraq and Afghanistan. This echoes concerns about US involvement in the conflict.

item.title: How Trump Event Shooting Became Conspiracy Magnet
item.description: A Trump event shooting sparked conspiracy theories with "staged" appearing in over 300,000 X posts by Sunday. The word "staged" appeared in more than 300,000 posts on X by midday Sunday.

item.title: China blocks Meta from acquiring AI startup Manus
item.description: China blocked Meta's acquisition of AI startup Manus, with Meta saying the transaction complied with applicable law and anticipating an appropriate resolution to the inquiry. The outcome of the block remains uncertain.

item.title: Epstein Trial In New Mexico Pushes Survivors To Reach Out To Authorities
item.description: Several individuals claim they were flown to Zorro Ranch in New Mexico and abused by Jeffrey Epstein. The allegations have prompted survivors to reach out to authorities, with an Epstein trial underway in the state.

item.title: Sri Lankan monks arrested after 110kg of cannabis discovered in their luggage
item.description: Sri Lankan monks were arrested after 110kg of cannabis was discovered in their luggage, with each monk found with around 5kg of the potent drug hidden in false walls. The monks were detained by officials.

item.title: Hezbollah Says It Will Keep Weapons as Lebanon Says Israeli Strikes Killed 14
item.description: Hezbollah leader Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah stated that they will keep their weapons. This comes as Israel and Hezbollah have been trading daily attacks despite a cease-fire, with 14 reported killed in recent Israeli strikes.

item.title: Alleged Correspondents' Dinner shooter to appear in court. And, Charles III visits U.S.
item.description: The suspect in the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting incident is set to appear in federal court today, while King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrive in Washington for a state visit.

item.title: Why Migrants’ Death Matters
item.description: Sciences Po published a report on migrants' deaths, highlighting their significance. The report likely examines the causes and consequences of migrant fatalities, but specific details are not provided. Why Migrants’ Death Matters Sciences Po.

item.title: Hezbollah leader rejects Lebanon-Israel direct talks, vows to confront Israel
item.description: Hezbollah leader rejected direct Lebanon-Israel talks, vowing to confront Israel. This stance comes after Lebanon and Israel's US ambassadors held meetings in Washington, the first of their kind in decades.

item.title: 'Glorious Leader?' OpenAI Co-Founder's Diary To Play Key Role In Musk Trial
item.description: Elon Musk's trial against OpenAI and Microsoft will feature the diary of OpenAI co-founder as key evidence. Musk is seeking $150 billion in damages from the two companies. Elon Musk is seeking $150 billion in damages from OpenAI and Microsoft.

item.title: Mali in turmoil after insurgents seize towns and kill defence minister
item.description: Mali's defence minister and military intelligence chief were killed in a weekend assault by jihadists and separatist rebels, who seized several towns and military bases. The attacks, coordinated by al-Qaida-affiliated groups, have left Mali reeling.

item.title: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
item.description: Colombia is offering a $1.4m reward for information on a rebel leader known as "Marlon", who is blamed for a deadly bomb attack that killed 20 people. The rebel leader allegedly ordered the attack.
